title: Machine Learning Engineering
subtitle: The Most Complete Applied AI Book on Building Reliable ML Solutions at Scale
author: Andriy Burkov
themes: ML engineering is fundamentally different from ML research
Data engineering is the long pole of every ML project
Reliability engineering matters more than peak accuracy
Mistakes are inevitable — design for detection and recovery
endorsements: Cassie Kozyrkov, Chief Decision Scientist at Google
Karolis Urbonas, Head of Machine Learning and Science at Amazon
excerpt: From the author of the world bestseller The Hundred-Page Machine Learning Book: the most complete applied AI book on building reliable machine learning solutions that scale. The book is organized in the order a successful project must actually be executed — starting with whether ML is the right tool, then domain understanding, data engineering, prototyping, evaluation, production, and finally reliability — and is filled with best practices and design patterns distilled from fifteen years of solving real problems with AI.
